results.studyTitle: Multifunctional self-driven origami paper-based integrated microfluidic chip to detect CRP and PAB in whole blood
results.hypothesis: A self-driven paper-based microfluidic biosensor functionalized for C-reactive protein detection will quantify CRP directly from whole blood without preprocessing in a point-of-care workflow, while preserving clinically useful sensitivity and turnaround time.

rawPlan.title: Rapid CRP paper-sensor validation package

parsedFields.label: Intervention
parsedFields.value: Paper-based electrochemical CRP biosensor with anti-CRP capture antibodies

parsedFields.label: Model system
parsedFields.value: Whole blood samples, no preprocessing workflow

parsedFields.label: Primary endpoint
parsedFields.value: Detection limit below 0.5 mg/L within 10 minutes

parsedFields.label: Mechanism
parsedFields.value: Electrochemical signal amplification from antibody-antigen binding event

parsedFields.label: Controls
parsedFields.value: Blank blood matrix, known CRP spike-ins, ELISA benchmark comparison

parsedFields.label: Readouts
parsedFields.value: Current response, calibration curve, time-to-result, ELISA concordance

references.title: Electrochemical paper biosensors for inflammatory marker detection in blood
references.note: Strong overlap on paper-electrode format and CRP target, but requires preprocessed plasma rather than raw whole blood.

references.title: Fabrication workflow for wax-patterned electrochemical paper devices
references.note: Useful scaffold for electrode printing, blocking, and capture antibody immobilization.

references.title: High-sensitivity CRP antibody pair application note
references.note: Supports reagent selection and calibration range assumptions used in the materials list.

protocol.title: Fabricate paper electrode strips
protocol.detail: Print working, reference, and counter electrodes on wax-patterned cellulose substrate, then cure and verify baseline resistance before functionalization.

protocol.title: Immobilize CRP capture chemistry
protocol.detail: Apply anti-CRP antibodies and blocking buffer to the sensing zone, then store under desiccation for overnight stabilization.

protocol.title: Run whole-blood spike-in panel
protocol.detail: Challenge strips with whole-blood samples across the expected CRP range without preprocessing and record electrochemical response at 2, 5, and 10 minutes.

protocol.title: Benchmark against ELISA
protocol.detail: Run matched CRP concentrations through a laboratory ELISA kit to estimate concordance, false negative rate, and lower limit of detection.

budget.note: Paper, carbon ink, wax patterning, blocking reagents
Capture chemistry and calibration standards
ELISA comparison kit and consumables
Potentiostat access and lab technician setup
Re-run buffer for strip defects or failed blocking

timeline.action: Finalize strip geometry, order antibodies, confirm potentiostat access
Fabricate and functionalize strips, verify baseline electrical behavior
Run CRP whole-blood spike-in matrix and optimize blocking chemistry
Benchmark against ELISA and prepare sensitivity summary

validation: Detection limit below 0.5 mg/L in whole blood within 10 minutes.
Signal-to-noise ratio must remain stable across at least three fabrication batches.
ELISA concordance target above 0.9 for matched calibration samples.

reviewFeedback.issue: Include anticoagulant-treated control blood source and transport constraints.
reviewFeedback.impact: Improves realism for same-day validation runs with fresh matrix samples.

reviewFeedback.issue: Specify humidity storage conditions for pre-functionalized strips.
reviewFeedback.impact: Reduces risk of degraded assay performance before testing.

reviewFeedback.issue: Add a hemolysis sensitivity check to whole-blood acceptance criteria.
reviewFeedback.impact: Makes the readout more trustworthy for real sample conditions.

results.studyTitle: An optimised electrochemical biosensor for the label-free detection of C-reactive protein in blood
results.hypothesis: An optimized label-free electrochemical immunosensor using anti-CRP capture chemistry on gold electrodes will detect C-reactive protein in blood with clinically relevant sensitivity and specificity suitable for translational assay development.

results.studyTitle: Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG treatment improves intestinal permeability and modulates microbiota dysbiosis in an experimental model of sepsis
results.hypothesis: Supplementing C57BL/6 mice with Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG for four weeks before challenge will reduce intestinal permeability relative to controls, measurable by FITC-dextran translocation and tight-junction protein preservation.

generated.title: Gut barrier reinforcement in murine probiotic dosing study

rawPlan.title: Gut barrier reinforcement in murine probiotic dosing study

parsedFields.label: Intervention
parsedFields.value: Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG, oral gavage for 4 weeks

parsedFields.label: Model system
parsedFields.value: C57BL/6 mice, 8 weeks old

parsedFields.label: Primary endpoint
parsedFields.value: FITC-dextran intestinal permeability reduction >= 30%

parsedFields.label: Mechanism
parsedFields.value: Upregulation of claudin-1 and occludin tight junction proteins

parsedFields.label: Controls
parsedFields.value: Vehicle control and baseline permeability readout

parsedFields.label: Readouts
parsedFields.value: FITC assay, qPCR, Western blot, body weight, stool score

references.title: Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG reduces epithelial barrier dysfunction in murine colitis models
references.note: Matches intervention and barrier-focused endpoint, but uses DSS injury instead of healthy mice.

references.title: FITC-dextran permeability assay in murine gut barrier studies
references.note: Provides the closest operational protocol for dosing, fasting, and sample timing.

references.title: Mouse tight junction protein quantification workflow for claudin-1 and occludin
references.note: Useful for validating antibody panel, extraction workflow, and storage assumptions.

protocol.title: Cohort setup and acclimation
protocol.detail: Randomize 24 female C57BL/6 mice into control and probiotic arms, then acclimate for 7 days under identical housing and chow conditions.

protocol.title: Daily probiotic administration
protocol.detail: Administer 1e9 CFU Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG in sterile PBS by oral gavage once daily for 28 consecutive days. Vehicle arm receives matched PBS volume.

protocol.title: Barrier challenge and serum collection
protocol.detail: Fast animals for 4 hours, dose FITC-dextran orally at 600 mg/kg, and collect serum 4 hours post dose for fluorescence quantification.

protocol.title: Mechanistic validation
protocol.detail: Harvest ileum and proximal colon, isolate RNA and protein, and quantify claudin-1 and occludin via qPCR and Western blot against GAPDH.

budget.note: Vendor order + 5 week husbandry
Includes 10% contingency on disposables
Plate reader, qPCR consumables, imaging
0.25 FTE research associate
Cold chain and rush shipping reserve

timeline.action: Protocol lock, supplier ordering, IACUC check
Cohort arrival, acclimation, baseline metadata capture
Daily probiotic dosing and welfare monitoring
FITC permeability assay and sample harvest
qPCR, Western blot, interpretation, review pack

validation: Primary success metric: serum FITC signal reduced by at least 30% versus control at day 29.
Mechanistic confirmation: claudin-1 and occludin expression trends move in the same direction by qPCR and Western blot.
Safety guardrails: no adverse body-weight trend beyond 10% and no worsening stool score.

reviewFeedback.issue: Dose concentration should be expressed per CFU per mouse, not per mL suspension.
reviewFeedback.impact: Prevents ambiguity during handoff to animal facility staff.

reviewFeedback.issue: Add frozen backup stock and shipping risk for live probiotic culture.
reviewFeedback.impact: Improves operational realism and avoids underpricing.

reviewFeedback.issue: Include stool score and body-weight trend as secondary tolerance checks.
reviewFeedback.impact: Strengthens interpretation if permeability improves but health markers worsen.

results.studyTitle: Freezing-induced uptake of trehalose into mammalian cells facilitates cryopreservation
results.hypothesis: Replacing conventional sugar additives with trehalose in a mammalian-cell freezing workflow will improve post-thaw survival by facilitating intracellular protection during freezing and thawing compared with standard cryopreservation conditions.

rawPlan.title: Trehalose cryopreservation viability study

parsedFields.label: Intervention
parsedFields.value: Trehalose-substituted freezing medium versus standard DMSO + sucrose

parsedFields.label: Model system
parsedFields.value: HeLa cells under matched cryopreservation and thawing workflow

parsedFields.label: Primary endpoint
parsedFields.value: Post-thaw viability improvement of at least 15 percentage points

parsedFields.label: Mechanism
parsedFields.value: Improved membrane stabilization during freezing stress

parsedFields.label: Controls
parsedFields.value: Standard DMSO freezing protocol and matched thaw timing

parsedFields.label: Readouts
parsedFields.value: Viability count, recovery at 24 hours, morphology, membrane integrity

references.title: Trehalose-based cryoprotection improves mammalian cell recovery after thaw
references.note: Relevant for trehalose substitution, but not specific to HeLa cells or direct sucrose comparison.

references.title: Standardized thaw-recovery viability assay for adherent cell lines
references.note: Useful reference for matched freezing, thawing, and 24-hour recovery assessment.

references.title: Cryoprotectant formulation considerations for DMSO alternatives
references.note: Supports media composition choices and thaw handling assumptions.

protocol.title: Prepare matched HeLa cultures
protocol.detail: Expand HeLa cells to log-phase growth, normalize passage number, and split into matched cryopreservation batches for each medium condition.

protocol.title: Formulate freezing media
protocol.detail: Prepare control medium with standard DMSO + sucrose and experimental medium with trehalose replacement at matched osmolarity.

protocol.title: Controlled-rate freezing
protocol.detail: Freeze equal-density aliquots using a controlled-rate freezing container, then transfer to long-term storage after the initial cooldown.

protocol.title: Thaw and assess recovery
protocol.detail: Thaw matched vials rapidly, plate standardized cell counts, and measure immediate viability and 24-hour recovery morphology.

budget.note: Cell line, media, flasks, and incubator use
Trehalose, sucrose, DMSO, and freezing containers
Cell counter, dyes, and imaging support
Bench time across prep, freeze, and thaw stages
Repeat vials for failed thaw conditions

timeline.action: Lock osmolarity targets, expand matched cell stock
Freeze both media conditions with controlled-rate handling
Thaw, count viability, and capture 24-hour recovery
Compare conditions, prepare recovery summary and review notes

validation: Immediate viability improvement of at least 15 percentage points versus control.
24-hour adherent recovery must not degrade despite higher initial viability.
Cell morphology and membrane integrity need to trend consistently with count data.

reviewFeedback.issue: Specify whether trehalose is extracellular only or also introduced via pre-loading.
reviewFeedback.impact: Clarifies a critical mechanistic assumption that affects reproducibility.

reviewFeedback.issue: Add recovery at 72 hours to detect delayed growth penalties.
reviewFeedback.impact: Prevents overestimating success from short-window viability alone.
Improves budget realism for labs that do not already stock them.

signals.label: Novelty signal
signals.hint: HeLa + direct sucrose swap remains useful and testable

signals.label: Planning horizon
signals.hint: Short cycle with delayed recovery checks

signals.label: Estimated budget
signals.hint: Cell culture and cryo workflow only

results.studyTitle: Performance of different Sporomusa species for the microbial electrosynthesis of acetate from carbon dioxide
results.hypothesis: Introducing Sporomusa ovata into a bioelectrochemical microbial electrosynthesis system will convert CO2 into acetate at a higher rate than alternative Sporomusa species under controlled cathode-driven conditions.

rawPlan.title: Sporomusa ovata carbon-fixation reactor plan

parsedFields.label: Intervention
parsedFields.value: Sporomusa ovata introduced into bioelectrochemical cathode system

parsedFields.label: Model system
parsedFields.value: Bench-scale electrochemical reactor at -400mV vs SHE

parsedFields.label: Primary endpoint
parsedFields.value: Acetate production >= 150 mmol/L/day and 20% above benchmark

parsedFields.label: Mechanism
parsedFields.value: Microbial CO2 fixation enhanced by electron uptake at cathode

parsedFields.label: Controls
parsedFields.value: Abiotic cathode, benchmark biocatalyst condition, open-circuit control

parsedFields.label: Readouts
parsedFields.value: Acetate titer, coulombic efficiency, current density, stability over time

references.title: Microbial electrosynthesis of acetate by Sporomusa ovata at poised cathodes
references.note: Strong mechanistic overlap, but benchmark rate target here is more aggressive than published median values.

references.title: Bench-scale bioelectrochemical reactor setup for acetogenic organisms
references.note: Operational reference for reactor assembly, gas handling, and startup sequence.

references.title: Carbon felt cathode handling and pretreatment guidance
references.note: Supports material selection and electrode pretreatment assumptions.

protocol.title: Assemble anaerobic reactor hardware
protocol.detail: Configure bench reactor with carbon felt cathode, reference electrode, gas-tight headspace, and potentiostat capable of stable -400mV vs SHE control.

protocol.title: Prepare inoculum and medium
protocol.detail: Revive Sporomusa ovata under anaerobic conditions, prepare bicarbonate-buffered medium, and confirm baseline growth before reactor inoculation.

protocol.title: Start electrosynthesis run
protocol.detail: Inoculate reactor, begin cathode poising, flow CO2 continuously, and monitor current density and acetate accumulation daily.

protocol.title: Benchmark productivity and stability
protocol.detail: Compare acetate rate, coulombic efficiency, and current stability against literature benchmark runs and abiotic controls.

budget.note: Electrodes, seals, reference electrode, tubing
Culture acquisition and anaerobic media preparation
Acetate kit, GC support, sampling consumables
Instrument access across multi-day runs
Leaks, failed inoculation, and additional seals

timeline.action: Confirm hardware availability and anaerobic workflow slots
Assemble reactor, pretreat electrodes, revive culture
Run electrosynthesis and capture daily productivity data
Benchmark output, assess stability, prepare decision memo

validation: Acetate productivity must meet or exceed 150 mmol/L/day.
Coulombic efficiency should remain within acceptable variance for at least three days of steady operation.
Abiotic control should confirm that acetate production is biologically mediated.

reviewFeedback.issue: Include startup lag expectations before declaring the run underperforming.
reviewFeedback.impact: Prevents premature failure calls during inoculum adaptation.

reviewFeedback.issue: Add gas manifold fittings and anaerobic sampling syringes.
reviewFeedback.impact: Closes a common operational gap in reactor planning.

reviewFeedback.issue: Track pH drift as a cofactor for acetate productivity interpretation.

signals.label: Novelty signal
signals.hint: Rate target is ambitious but grounded
